sections.paragraph:
ὄντως adv. of the ptc. ὤν (Eur., X., Pla.+; loanw. in rabb.) pert. to being actually so, really, certainly, in truth

ⓐ lit., as adv. (PGiss 22, 6; Num 22:37; Jer 3:23; Jos., Bell. 1, 68; Just., D. 74, 2; Ath. 23, 4 τὸ ὄντως ὄν) Lk 23:47; 24:34; J 8:36; 1 Cor 14:25; Gal 3:21; PtK 4 p. 16, 5. εἶχον τὸν Ἰωάννην ὄν. ὅτι προφήτης ἦν they held that John was really a prophet Mk 11:32.

ⓑ Used attributively (Pla., Phdr. 260a, Clit. 409e; SIG 893b, 8f τὸν ὄν. Ἡρακλείδην; PStras 53 in Novae Comoed. Fgm. ed. OSchröder=Kl. T. 135 p. 47, 14 ὄν. θεός; Sb 3924, 39; Philo, Poster. Cai. 167; Jos., Ant. 15, 63 οἱ ὄν. βασιλεῖς; Ar. 4, 1 τοῦ ὄν. θεοῦ Just., Ath.; Schwyzer II 415) ἡ ὄν. χήρα the real widow (one without resources, in contrast to one who has resources, such as relatives, or is still of marriageable age, or, as some hold, has been married several times) 1 Ti 5:3, 5, 16. ἡ ὄν. ζωή real, true life 6:19. Opp. ὁ ὄν. θάνατος Dg 10:7. οἱ ὄν. ἀποφεύγοντες 2 Pt 2:18 v.l.—DELG s.v. εἰμί. M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

ptc. ptc. = participle

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

PGiss PGiss = Griechische Papyri . . . zu Giessen—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

PtK PtK = Petruskerygma (Preaching of Peter)—List 1

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PStras PStras = Griechische Papyrus der kaiserlichen Universitäts- und Landesbibliothek zu Strassburg—List 4

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

Kl. T. Kl. T. = Kleine Texte, ed. HLietzmann, also KAland—List 6

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Schwyzer Schwyzer = ES., Griechische Grammatik—List 6

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 715
